entry.title: Washington Post: 2 Aug, 2022
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Pelosi visit is a test for Taiwan’s global status under Chinese pressure
  • World is ‘one miscalculation away from nuclear annihilation,’ U.N. says
  • BP profits highest in 14 years, raking in $8.5B as consumers feel gas pump pinch
  • Brittney Griner back in Russian court amid hopes of a prisoner swap
  • Ukraine Live Briefing: Promising signs for grain shipments from Odessa; Griner returns to Russian court
  • Taiwan braces for Chinese show of force, with Pelosi set to visit
  • Killing of Zawahiri draws praise from bipartisan lawmakers, Saudi Arabia
  • What Ayman al-Zawahiri’s killing means for al-Qaeda
  • U.S. agency warns of Afghan famine, more suppression of women’s rights
  • Ukraine Live Briefing: First grain shipment leaves Ukraine as Zelesnky urges security for future food exports
  • White House warns China not to overreact to expected Pelosi visit to Taiwan
  • Here are the al-Qaeda and ISIS leaders killed in U.S. strikes or raids
  • What do Ukraine’s new wheat exports mean for prices and global hunger?
  • Ayman al-Zawahiri, al-Qaeda leader, killed at 71
  • What’s at stake with a Pelosi visit to Taiwan
  • Here’s why top U.S. officials like Nancy Pelosi don’t usually go to Taiwan
  • First ship carrying grain leaves Odessa in deal to ease global food crisis
  • Ukrainian youth plan cleanup raves
  • Germany is firing up old coal plants, sparking fears climate goals will go up in smoke
  • Tensions between Kosovo and Serbia: Why NATO is ‘prepared to intervene’
  • Pakistan says army general, 5 others die in helicopter crash
  • Taliban under scrutiny as US kills al-Qaida leader in Kabul
  • Italy's salty Po Delta hurting agriculture, fisheries
  • Iran arrests Baha'i members; advocates demand their release
  • Tropical Storm Frank weakening off Mexico's Pacific coast
  • 10-year-old is last Uvalde victim to leave the hospital, 66 days later
  • Beyoncé used ‘ableist’ slur in a new song. After uproar, she’s deleting it.
  • Kentucky judge revives state abortion ban, reversing lower-court order
  • After flooding, a Kentucky church offers shelter alongside prayers
  • 2 die in McKinney Fire, now California’s largest wildfire this year
  • Veterans call rejection of toxic-exposure bill ‘a slap in the face’
  • An 18-year-old lifeguard helped deliver a baby on a YMCA pool deck
  • A reporter went to cover an execution. She was told her skirt was too short.
  • Memo shows Wis. GOP lawyer privately opposed decertifying Biden’s 2020 win
  • Post Politics Now: Biden says ‘justice has been delivered’ with killing of al-Qaeda’s Zawahiri
  • First Jan. 6 defendant convicted at trial receives longest sentence of 7 years
  • Biden covid case highlights confusing CDC guidance on ending isolation
  • W.Va. politician who apologized for Jan. 6 now writing defiant book
  • BP profits highest in 14 years, as it rakes in $8.5B amid gas pump pinch
  • Visa could be liable in suit over child sexual abuse material on Pornhub, other sites
  • Government workers feel inflation’s pinch as wages lag
  • Your gadgets die quickly by design. It doesn’t have to be that way.
  • Gadget graveyard: We found the hidden death dates on popular devices
  • Twitter is probing Elon Musk’s social circle in broad legal requests
  • A personalized — and free — service for solving your DIY woes
  • Installing a floor is harder than you think. Here’s how to do it right.
  • A road map to homeownership for consumers with thin credit files
  • It’s Far Too Risky to Assume That the Bottom Is In
  • Union Bashing Won’t Win It for the Tories
  • Red America Should Love Green Energy Spending
  • Got a Labor Shortage? Make It Easier to Work
  • Why The World’s Oldest Sovereign Fund Is in Turmoil: QuickTake
  • What Will Be Different About Italy’s Snap Elections
  • What Experts Know About ‘Long Covid’ and Who Gets It
  • Understanding the Unraveling of Tunisia’s Revolution: QuickTake
  • Biden might be the Democrats’ worst possible nominee (except for all the others)
  • The U.S. is bad at handling epidemics. Monkeypox is the latest example.
  • Our third party is not a party of ideas! It is the idea of a party!
  • Zawahiri was in ‘downtown Kabul’ because of Biden’s disastrous Afghanistan withdrawal
  • Kyrsten Sinema has all the power right now. She should use it.
  • Biden has tested positive again. That’s no reason to avoid Paxlovid.
  • Did Russia or Ukraine slaughter Ukrainian POWs? It’s not a close call.
  • 3 Senate races show how MAGA candidates are dragging down the GOP
  • ‘Fox & Friends,’ at its transparent best
  • The ticking time bomb still threatening the big climate deal
  • A promise Joe Biden should break
  • What $50 billion in taxpayer aid for airlines did not fix
  • The big guy’s little thugs
  • Ask Eugene Robinson about the latest news around the U.S. and world
  • Chat with Perry Bacon Jr. about politics and beyond
  • Chat with Jennifer Rubin about her columns, politics, policy and more
  • Need a dose of humor in your day? Chat with Alexandra Petri.
Breaking news and analysis on politics, business, world national news, entertainment more. In-depth DC, Virginia, Maryland news coverage including traffic, weather, crime, education, restaurant reviews and more.

entry.title: NY Times: 2 Aug, 2022
entry.content:

entry.content:

entry.title: South China Morning Post: 2 Aug, 2022
entry.summary: South China Morning Post: 2 Aug, 2022 published at 2022-08-02 11:00AM.
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • HSBC executives face off with Hong Kong shareholders in charm offensive to fend off calls to split city’s largest bank
  • Firefighters called to douse flaming Ferrari sports car on Hong Kong highway
  • Hong Kong police seize HK$63 million worth of drugs, arrest 2 suspects trying to get into car used for storing cocaine
  • Hong Kong activist detained for inciting subversion of state wins judicial review to lift reporting restrictions on case
  • Hong Kong retail sales continue to decline, falling 1.2 per cent in June
  • Coronavirus: Hong Kong approves Sinovac jab for toddlers as officials negotiate for BioNTech’s tailored vaccine formula
  • Coronavirus: recovered Hongkongers should still get three shots of Covid-19 vaccine, says expert

  • Hong Kong national security police arrest immigration officer, government technician over online messages
  • Hong Kong education authorities reject survival plans by 2 under-enrolled secondary schools
  • Car dealers propose easing warranty restrictions on maintenance, repair services after probe by Hong Kong’s competition watchdog

  • Taiwan Strait on the boil as US, Beijing and Taipei put military on high alert
  • Jet that carried Nancy Pelosi to Malaysia leaves Kuala Lumpur, with Beijing on alert over potential Taiwan stop
  • Tencent and NetEase video game drought continues as China approves another 69 titles, mostly from small developers

  • ‘Confident superpower or insecure?’ Beijing’s reaction if Pelosi goes to Taiwan may be telling
  • China’s state-run semiconductor fund engulfed in scandal as Beijing probes three senior executives for alleged corruption
  • China-backed Nairobi Expressway opens in Kenya, paving way for more cautious financing model
  • China can afford to abandon its GDP target, but not its promise of economic growth
  • Massive RCEP trade deal fails in areas where smaller bilateral pacts in Asia-Pacific shine
  • British MPs plan visit to Taiwan as tension with China simmers: report
  • China may grant Brazilian soymeal export permits in two months
  • Nancy Pelosi will visit Taiwan, reports say, as China vows ‘strong measures’ in response
  • Pelosi, Taiwan and climate: why military conflict might be good for the planet
  • Tech war: China’s semiconductor ambitions face reality check amid reports US is set to widen ban on exports of equipment to country
  • Pelosi’s likely Taiwan plans cast shadow over PLA’s 95th year celebrations
  • Taiwan may try to keep high-level Pelosi visit ‘low-profile’, analysts say
  • Zambia cancels US$1.6 billion Chinese loans and halts infrastructure projects in move to avoid debt crisis
  • Singaporean accused of killing wife with pillow told UK police he wanted her to stop ‘nagging’
  • US used Pakistani airspace for drone that killed al-Qaeda chief Ayman al-Zawahri: analysts
  • Pakistan election agency rules former Prime Minister Imran Khan’s party received illegal funds
  • Australia fines traveller from Indonesia nearly US$2,000 for undeclared McMuffins: ‘Biosecurity is no joke’
  • Is South Korea’s Yoon Suk-yeol seeking to crush opponents with new police bureau?
  • US envoy to Japan seeks to counter China’s ‘economic coercion’ with technology alliance
  • North Korean hackers are posing as crypto freelancers to secure remote work abroad, researchers say
  • Singapore hawkers feel the sting of rising prices, ‘no thanks to Russia’
  • Philippines’ Fidel Ramos sought Beijing ties but also showed South China Sea defiance
  • Elizabeth Zhong murder: Chinese millionaire Fang Sun jailed for more than 18 years
  • Shinzo Abe assassination: Japan ruling party says parliamentary eulogy for ex-PM postponed
  • India investigates Asia’s first suspected moneypox-linked death
  • Brittney Griner appears in Russian court on cannabis charge after US makes swap offer
  • US accuses Russia of using Ukraine’s Zaporizhzhia power plant as ‘nuclear shield’
  • US watched al-Qaeda chief Zawahiri for months before ‘tailored air strike’ on Kabul hideout
  • Climate change: possible human extinction a ‘dangerously underexplored topic’, scientists say
  • Conjoined twins separated in 27-hour surgery with help of virtual reality
  • Did US use secret ‘ninja’ missile to kill al-Qaeda leader Zawahiri?
  • Big tech should share Europe network costs, say France, Italy, Spain, but some fear threat to net neutrality
  • US to send US$550 million of weapons and ammunition to Ukraine forces, official says
  • US kills al-Qaeda chief Ayman al-Zawahiri in Kabul drone strike
  • US Senate likely to delay bill on gay marriage until September
  • 80 men appear in South Africa court for gang rape of 8 women during music video shoot
  • UN chief Antonio Guterres warns world is one step away from ‘nuclear annihilation’
  • Vatican says Pope Francis to visit Kazakhstan; expected to meet Russian Orthodox patriarch who justified Ukraine invasion
  • UK PM race: Foreign Secretary Liz Truss wins cabinet backing as voting begins to elect new Conservative leader
  • Chinese stocks get cheaper for money managers who don’t want to be late for economy reopening party
  • What buyers of uncompleted properties overseas from Hong Kong should do to protect their interests
  • Alibaba, HSBC, AIA pace rout in Hong Kong on Taiwan tensions as China promises ‘firm and strong’ measures against Pelosi’s visit
  • Australia Foreign Investment Review Board fee hike is likely to weigh on home prices, property investment, analysts say
  • Chinese EV maker Nio planning mass market foray with new brand, to challenge Toyota and Volkswagen with compact cars priced below 200,000 yuan
  • Hong Kong can tap IPO potential of tech start-ups with innovative listing reforms
  • China’s top EV trio suffer sales setback in July as heatwave deters consumers but new models inspire second-half confidence
  • TikTok’s e-commerce push heats up in Southeast Asia with new shopping campaigns as retail initiatives in the West hit a snag
  • The personal style that makes Martha Stewart a fashion influencer, and her streetwear line – she’s turning 81 on a roll
  • Far Far Away movie review: Hong Kong’s remote fringes and culture explored in Amos Why’s quirky romantic comedy
  • What are hiccups and how to make them stop? Eating sugar, drinking water backwards? Experts on their causes and cures
  • Tonga reopens borders after 2-year Covid-19 closure, and returning islanders cry tears of joy
  • Blackpink drop new song, announce upcoming album Born Pink and world tour, marking K-pop girl group’s long-awaited comeback
  • Chinese recipes and ‘stories of our culture’ in Chinese-Ish – but don’t think of it as a playbook for authentic Asian cuisine, say its co-authors
  • K-pop star Kang Daniel on his SpongeBob SquarePants collaboration, upcoming solo concert debut and his music company Konnect
  • Chiang Kai-shek used Western journalists to promote his Nationalist cause abroad – including a controversial Australian radio host
  • The Sandman, Netflix comic-book fantasy series, is out on August 5 – everything you need to know about the Neil Gaiman DC Comics adaptation
  • Inside Donnie Yen’s crazy rich life: as a Hong Kong martial arts icon and one of Asia’s highest-paid actors, the John Wick 4 actor splurges on BMWs, Rolexes – and a US$17 million mansion for his wife
  • What’s changed for Ermenegildo Zegna after going public? The Italian luxury fashion brand listed on the NYSE in 2021 … but remains very much a family company
  • Why is Hermès skipping out on the second-hand luxury market? Like LVMH, the Birkin bag maker is disinterested in sustainable, pre-owned fashion platforms
  • Meet Kang Tae-oh, breakout star in Extraordinary Attorney Woo: he made his K-pop debut with 5urprise before landing K-drama Run On with SNSD’s Sooyung – and went to the same uni as BTS’ Jin
  • Ariana Grande’s 20 most iconic fashion looks ever: inside her style transformation from Nickelodeon teen starlet to pop princess donning Versace, Alexander McQueen and Giambattista Valli
SCMP.com, Hong Kong's premier online English daily provides exclusive up-to-date news, audio video news, podcasts, RSS Feeds, Blogs, breaking news, top stories, award winning news and analysis on Hong Kong and China.

entry.title: Sydney Morning Herald: 2 Aug, 2022
entry.summary: Sydney Morning Herald: 2 Aug, 2022 published at 2022-08-02 11:00AM.
entry.content:
Articles in this issue:
  • Putting the horse before the opera
  • Winners of hit new reality show Hunted plan to use prize money to start families
  • Cunnington set to return after year out; Grimes racing the clock; The motivation behind Cumberland’s performance

  • ‘Last year, the FBI foiled a kidnap plot. Now a killer is dispatched to deal with me’
  • Gallery: The best photos from around the world
  • ‘Justice has been delivered’: Al-Qaeda leader Ayman al-Zawahiri killed in US airstrike

  • Eagle eyes are on Taiwan, but is China distracting us from a ‘second Pearl Harbour’?
  • I’ve had it with the unbearable whiteness of teeth
  • ‘The siren has sounded’: Scientists pinpoint COVID’s origin
  • As it happened: RBA hikes interest rates to 1.85 per cent; COVID experts say winter wave appears to have peaked
  • Appen shares plummet to lowest point in almost five years after earnings drop
  • So much for the soft landing for house prices. It’s now a painful thud

  • The Wrap: ASX up for sixth straight day as RBA raises interest rates
  • The RBA weapon that will help push rates up further

  • Facebook must ditch Zuckerberg if it is to prosper

  • Gas giants hold emergency talks as minister threatens intervention

The Asahi Shimbun is widely regarded for its journalism as the most respected daily newspaper in Japan. The English version offers selected articles from the vernacular Asahi Shimbun, as well as extensive coverage of cool Japan,focusing on manga, travel and other timely news.
